subject: [PATCH v4 2/5] mm: convert zone lock users to wrappers

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch should follow the existing coding style, specifically using two tabs for indentation on second parameter lines
reviews.raw_body:
We tend to use two-tabs on second parameter line; like the existing code
did.


Besides that

Acked-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the locking in mm/page_alloc.c correctly and requested a fix to drop the lock before calling try_to_unmap().
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>


Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-02-27.patch_summary: This patch replaces direct zone lock acquire and release operations in various kernel modules with newly introduced wrappers. The changes are mechanical substitutions only, without any functional changes or modifications to locking semantics or ordering. This is the second of five patches aimed at converting zone lock users to wrappers.

2026-02-28.patch_summary: This patch adds tracepoint instrumentation to zone lock acquire and release operations in the Linux kernel. The implementation uses a lightweight inline helper to check if tracing is enabled, and calls an out-of-line helper when tracing is active. When CONFIG_TRACING is disabled, the helpers compile to empty inline stubs, leaving the fast path unaffected. This patch introduces three new tracepoints: zone_lock_start_locking, zone_lock_acquire_returned, and zone_lock_released.

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.raw_body:
On 2/27/26 17:00, Dmitry Ilvokhin wrote:
> Replace direct zone lock acquire/release operations with the
> newly introduced wrappers.
> 
> The changes are purely mechanical substitutions. No functional change
> intended. Locking semantics and ordering remain unchanged.
> 
> The compaction path is left unchanged for now and will be
> handled separately in the following patch due to additional
> non-trivial modifications.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
> Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
> Reviewed-by: SeongJae Park <sj@kernel.org>

Reviewed-by: Vlastimil Babka (SUSE) <vbabka@kernel.org>

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch replaces direct zone lock acquire and release operations in various kernel modules with newly introduced wrappers. The changes are mechanical substitutions only, without any functional changes or modifications to locking semantics or ordering. This is the second of five patches aimed at converting zone lock users to wrappers.

2026-03-03.patch_summary:
Replace direct zone lock acquire/release operations with the newly introduced wrappers.

The changes are purely mechanical substitutions. No functional change intended. Locking semantics and ordering remain unchanged.

The compaction path is left unchanged for now and will be handled separately in the following patch due to additional non-trivial modifications.

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch adds tracepoint instrumentation to zone lock acquire and release operations in the Linux kernel. The implementation uses a lightweight inline helper to check if tracing is enabled, and calls an out-of-line helper when tracing is active. When CONFIG_TRACING is disabled, the helpers compile to empty inline stubs, leaving the fast path unaffected. This patch introduces three new tracepoints: zone_lock_start_locking, zone_lock_acquire_returned, and zone_lock_released.

2026-03-05.patch_summary: This patch adds tracepoint instrumentation to zone lock acquire and release operations in the Linux kernel. The implementation uses a lightweight inline helper to check if tracing is enabled, and calls an out-of-line helper when tracing is active. When CONFIG_TRACING is disabled, the helpers compile to empty inline stubs, leaving the fast path unaffected. This patch introduces three new tracepoints: zone_lock_start_locking, zone_lock_acquire_returned, and zone_lock_released.
